blocks.content: Thursday. 7:02am. I saw her at the corner near the senior lockers.
She was reading something. Not on her phone — an actual book. She had her back to the wall and one foot up, and she looked like she'd been standing there her whole life.
I saw her at the corner near the senior lockers and walked the other way.
I saw her at the corner near the senior lockers. Kept walking. Tryouts first. I told myself that was the reason.
I saw her at the corner near the senior lockers. Stopped. Didn't move. By the time I did she was already walking.
She said it first. Like she'd been waiting for me to be the one who didn't walk past.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Hey. What you said in class yesterday — about the narrator. That was real.
She smiled. Not wide. Just — enough.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Good luck today. I heard you're trying out.
She knew. She'd been paying attention too.
I thought about going back. I didn't.
I thought about going back. I didn't.
Jonathan would have said something. I know that. He wouldn't have even thought about it.
